prompt:
I'm building the caching layer for our edge nodes and I refuse to sprinkle freshness math across the codebase again. I want one pure decision-table module, freshness.ts — no sockets, no Date.now(), every function takes the times it needs. TypeScript, node stdlib only. HTTP dates are IMF-fixdate strings ('Tue, 01 Apr 2025 12:00:00 GMT'); all header maps use lowercase names; times are epoch milliseconds; durations from headers are seconds and get converted.

A stored response is { status, headers, storedAtMs }.

Exports:

1. parseCacheControl(value?) → { maxAge, sMaxage (null when absent), noCache, noStore, public, private, mustRevalidate, immutable (booleans), extensions }. Directive names case-insensitive; values may be quoted; whitespace around commas and '=' is tolerated. Seconds values must be non-negative integers — anything else drops that directive. If a directive repeats, the FIRST occurrence wins. Unknown directives go into extensions verbatim (value string, or true for bare tokens).

2. freshnessLifetimeMs(resp, opts?) with opts { shared?, heuristicFraction? }. Precedence: s-maxage (only when shared) → max-age → (Expires − Date) → heuristic. Expires uses the Date header as its baseline, falling back to storedAtMs when Date is missing; a past or unparseable Expires means a lifetime of 0 (but only when no max-age outranks it). The heuristic applies only when status is one of 200, 203, 301, 404, 410 and Last-Modified is present: fraction (default 0.10) of (Date − Last-Modified), floored at 0 and capped at 24 hours. Nothing at all → 0.

3. currentAgeMs(resp, nowMs) — RFC 9111 flavored: apparentAge = max(0, storedAtMs − dateValue) (dateValue falls back to storedAtMs when Date is missing/unparseable); correctedAge = max(apparentAge, Age header in seconds, digits only, ignored when malformed); age = correctedAge + (nowMs − storedAtMs).

4. evaluateCached(resp, requestHeaders, nowMs, opts { shared? }) → { action: 'serve' | 'revalidate' | 'fetch', conditionalHeaders? }. The table, in order: response no-store OR request no-store → fetch. shared && response private → fetch. Response no-cache, or request no-cache → revalidate when the stored response has validators, else fetch. Otherwise fresh (lifetime > age, strictly — equal is stale) → serve. Stale → revalidate with validators, fetch without. conditionalHeaders exists only for revalidate: 'if-none-match' echoing the stored etag verbatim (W/ prefix and all) and/or 'if-modified-since' echoing the stored last-modified string verbatim — both when both exist. serve/fetch decisions must not carry conditionalHeaders.

5. evaluateConditional(requestHeaders, current, method = 'GET') → { status: 200 | 304 | 412 } for the origin side. current is { etag?, lastModifiedMs? }. If-None-Match, when present, is authoritative and If-Modified-Since is then IGNORED: parse it as a comma-separated list of entity-tags — commas INSIDE quoted tags must not split (ETags like "v2,final" are legal) — compare weakly (strip W/ from both sides, compare the quoted opaque part), '*' matches iff a current representation (etag) exists. On a match: 304 for GET/HEAD, 412 for anything else. No match → 200. Without If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since applies to GET/HEAD only: unparseable date or unknown lastModifiedMs → 200; lastModified <= the header's time → 304; newer → 200.

node --test test_freshness.ts is the contract. Don't touch the test file.
